name: Geocities Philippines
description: The original home of Filipino personal pages — glitter text, MIDI music, and 'Under Construction' GIFs included.
content_note: Personal homepages, fan pages, and hobby sites built by ordinary Filipinos.

name: PinoyExchange
description: The internet's version of a Filipino town plaza — where OFWs argued about politics at midnight and the Buhay Pinoy thread became a decade-long national diary.
content_note: Forum threads covering politics, entertainment, relationships, and overseas Filipino life.

name: Friendster Philippines
description: Before Facebook rewired the world, Friendster was where Filipinos built their first digital social lives — complete with glittery profile layouts and Testimonials from barkada.
content_note: User profiles, testimonials, photo albums, and group pages.

name: Multiply Philippines
description: The Filipino blogger's social network of choice — where barrio-level gossip, ukay-ukay finds, and homemade food businesses first went online.
content_note: Blogs, photo albums, online stores, and personal journals.

name: Philippine Daily Inquirer Online (INQ7)
description: The Inquirer's original web presence before it became inquirer.net — a snapshot of how Filipino news first moved online.
content_note: News articles, opinion columns, and early Philippine online journalism.

name: ABS-CBN Interactive
description: When the country's biggest TV network first went online — early showbiz news, teleserye updates, and the birth of the Filipino online fandom.
content_note: Entertainment news, show pages, celebrity profiles, and early streaming attempts.

name: Ragnarok Online Philippines
description: Ground zero for the Philippine gaming cafe boom — where thousands of Filipino students spent their baon hunting Porings after school.
content_note: Game updates, guild boards, patch notes, and community events.

name: Level Up! Games Philippines
description: The publisher that brought Ragnarok, RF Online, and a generation of Filipino gamers their first online adventures.
content_note: Game portals, character creation guides, server status, and top player rankings.

name: Star Cinema Online
description: Where Filipino movie fans tracked their favorite love teams before Twitter made real-time fandom possible.
content_note: Movie listings, trailers, actor profiles, and Filipino box office updates.

name: OPM Philippines Fan Sites
description: The scrappy fan-built corners of the early web where Filipino music lovers shared lyrics, tabs, and love for Original Pilipino Music.
content_note: Song lyrics, guitar tabs, artist profiles, and early OPM mp3 sharing.

name: Philippine Gaming Zone
description: A grassroots gaming community built by Filipino players, for Filipino players — tips, cheats, and guild drama all in Taglish.
content_note: Game guides, forums, guild recruitment, and tournament announcements.

name: Pinoy Web Awards
description: The early internet's attempt to catalogue and celebrate the best Filipino websites — a digital hall of fame for the first generation of Pinoy web builders.
content_note: Website directories, awards listings, and early Philippine web community news.

name: E-Heads Online
description: The official and fan sites of the Beatles of Philippine rock — where Huling El Bimbo still plays in the background of someone's GeoCities tribute page.
content_note: Discography, lyrics, fan tributes, and concert announcements.

name: Globe Telecom Early Portal
description: Back when loading the Globe homepage on dial-up was itself a small victory — the early face of Philippine mobile internet.
content_note: Prepaid load promos, SMS services, early WAP content, and corporate news.

name: Philippine Science High School Online
description: One of the first Philippine school websites — a window into how academic institutions tentatively stepped into the internet age.
content_note: School announcements, academic programs, alumni pages, and early student web projects.

name: Yehey
description: The premier local search engine and web portal, often dubbed the 'Yahoo! of the Philippines'.
content_note: Search engine, web directory, news, and free email.

name: Chikka
description: An essential internet-to-mobile SMS application that connected Filipinos and OFWs for free.
content_note: Free text messaging service and early mobile social networking.

name: Sulit
description: The undisputed king of local classified ads before being acquired and rebranded to OLX.
content_note: Buy and sell classifieds, real estate, and automotive listings.

name: PhilMusic
description: The absolute hub for the local Original Pilipino Music (OPM) and independent music scene.
content_note: Message boards, gig schedules, and band promotions.

name: Titik Pilipino
description: The go-to exhaustive database for Philippine music, lyrics, and guitar chords.
content_note: Song lyrics, album reviews, artist profiles, and charts.

name: EDSAMail
description: A brilliant early local free email service optimized for slow dial-up connections.
content_note: Webmail client and local news updates.

name: AyosDito
description: A massive classified ads platform and the main competitor to Sulit.com.ph.
content_note: Online marketplace for second-hand goods and services.

name: Mukamo
description: A wildly popular Filipino forum and blog aggregating local news and TV show streams.
content_note: Message boards, teleserye streaming links, and gossip.

name: Tsinoy
description: A prominent niche community portal catering specifically to the Chinese-Filipino community.
content_note: Forums, dating sections, events, and lifestyle discussions.

name: PinoyCentral
description: An entertainment and news portal launched by ABS-CBN to rival Yehey.
content_note: Celebrity gossip, pop culture updates, and network news.

name: Peyups
description: The ultimate online hangout for University of the Philippines (UP) students and alumni.
content_note: Campus debates, gossip, and 'eyeball' meetups.

name: Symbianize
description: A massive grey-market forum dedicated to tech workarounds, free internet tricks, and file sharing.
content_note: Tech support, modding guides, and pirated software.

name: TristanCafe
description: A popular community forum known for its chatrooms, e-poetry, and digital sanctuary vibe for teens.
content_note: Chatrooms, poetry boards, and flash games.

name: FemaleNetwork / GirlTalk
description: The premier safe space for Filipinas to discuss relationships, careers, beauty, and local showbiz tea.
content_note: Message boards on lifestyle, beauty, and career.

name: PinoyWap
description: A text-heavy mobile portal that allowed Filipinos to download ringtones and chat on basic feature phones.
content_note: WAP forums, ringtones, and pixel wallpapers.

name: PinoyPC
description: The premier gathering spot for Filipino PC builders and hardware enthusiasts.
content_note: Tech support, overclocking advice, and buy/sell boards for parts.

name: Pacland
description: Manny Pacquiao’s unofficial-turned-official fan forum during the height of his boxing career.
content_note: Boxing analysis, fight schedules, and fan debates.

name: PhilBored
description: An early lifestyle forum catering to yuppies and college students, centered around the Manila nightlife scene.
content_note: Nightlife events, music, and socializing.

name: PinoyUnderground
description: An edgy, counter-culture forum discussing topics considered taboo on mainstream sites.
content_note: Underground music, hacking, and unfiltered political debates.

name: PinoyBoard
description: A general discussion forum that peaked during the early dial-up days.
content_note: General discussions and community bonding.

name: Uzzap
description: A wildly popular PC and mobile chat application and portal backed by Smart Communications.
content_note: Chat rooms, avatars, and private messaging.

name: Moko.mobi
description: A mobile-first social network targeting the youth for photo uploads and WAP chatting.
content_note: Mobile photo sharing and community walls.

name: I.ph
description: A local free blogging platform launched by dotPH giving Filipinos their first personalized local domain.
content_note: Personal online diaries and blog networks.

name: Blogr.ph
description: An aggregator and social network specifically built for Filipino bloggers.
content_note: Blog traffic sharing and content cross-promotion.

name: PinoyTumblr
description: A community hub that aggregated the works of Filipino creators on Tumblr.
content_note: Art, photography, and writing from local creators.

name: e-Games Philippines
description: A massive gaming portal that hosted hits like CABAL Online, O2Jam, and Audition Dance Battle.
content_note: Game portals, event news, and digital top-ups.

name: Gameclub
description: The portal that dominated the local FPS scene in internet cafes, hub for Crossfire and Weapons of War.
content_note: Game patches, esports updates, and community forums.

name: O2Jam Philippines
description: The standalone local domain for the incredibly popular music rhythm game featuring local OPM tracks.
content_note: Patch updates, song lists, and player rankings.

name: CandyMag / TeenTalk Forums
description: The iconic TeenTalk forums where teenage girls discussed crushes, school, and fashion.
content_note: Teen lifestyle articles and active message boards.

name: GameX
description: A platform launched by Smart Communications for buying digital pins and in-game currency via mobile load.
content_note: Digital storefront for gaming credits.

name: FHM Philippines
description: The digital home of the Philippines' best-selling men’s magazine with active forums and '100 Sexiest' voting.
content_note: Editorial shoots, pop culture articles, and forums.

name: UnoMagazine
description: The online hub for FHM's main rival, featuring edgy editorial content and pop culture reviews.
content_note: Lifestyle articles, gear reviews, and editorials.

name: K-Zone Philippines
description: The interactive website for the beloved kids and teens magazine, filled with flash games and anime news.
content_note: Flash games, anime forums, and geek culture.

name: HeroTV
description: The official website of ABS-CBN’s defunct anime cable channel.
content_note: Anime schedules, dubbing news, and fan forums.

name: AnimePinoy
description: A dedicated hub for Pinoy otakus to discuss local anime dubs and organize cosplay events.
content_note: Cosplay updates, peer-to-peer sharing, and forum discussions.

name: Flippish
description: The local destination for professionally produced, comedic, and edgy viral Filipino video content before YouTube exploded.
content_note: Sketch comedy, viral videos, and web series.

name: PugadBaboy
description: The early official domain for Pol Medina Jr.’s legendary comic strip.
content_note: Digital comic archives and merchandise.

name: Odysseylive
description: The digital offshoot of the Odyssey record store chain aiming for local music streaming.
content_note: Music downloads, artist interviews, and streaming.

name: OPM2Go
description: An ambitious digital music storefront championed by Ogie Alcasid, aiming to be the 'iTunes of OPM'.
content_note: Digital music sales and artist promotion.

name: MyMusicStore
description: A localized digital music download store offering exclusive local tracks to combat music piracy.
content_note: MP3 downloads, local charts, and music news.

name: GMANews.tv
description: GMA Network's standalone news domain following the INQ7 split before folding into the main corporate domain.
content_note: News articles, broadcast clips, and public affairs.

name: Trabaho
description: One of the absolute first job portals in the Philippines, highly popular in the early 2000s.
content_note: Job listings, resume uploads, and career advice.

name: Local.com.ph
description: A directory and web portal that attempted to compete directly with Yehey!.
content_note: Free email, local news aggregation, and search functionalities.

name: Wika
description: A localized search engine and web directory that categorized Filipino websites.
content_note: Search functions and localized web catalogues.

name: T3 Philippines
description: The digital wing of the local T3 Magazine franchise serving as a primary tech review site.
content_note: Gadget reviews, tech news, and gaming console specs.
